one_line_takeaway: By modeling 37 human-readable prompt features as causal (not merely correlational) drivers of jailbreak success via joint LLM+GNN causal discovery, the paper shows targeted feature amplification boosts attack success rate (RI up to +143.36%) beyond what prompt-length alone explains, and that exposing the same causal graph to a defense model improves malicious-intent extraction (ROUGE-Avg 21.67 vs 16.23) over a non-causal baseline.
key_finding: Causal-Analyst's learned causal graph achieves a targeted 12.07% edge connectivity (213 edges) -- sparser than DirectLiNGAM (21.60%, 381 edges) but denser than PC (9.86%, 174 edges) -- and the shuffled-label control collapses structural consistency (SHD=135, edges drop 213->78, classification F1 drops from 0.8108 to 0.6611), supporting that the graph captures real associations rather than artifacts. 'Positive Character' and 'Number of Task Steps' emerge as recurring direct causal drivers of Answer Harmfulness (Table VII). Using these causal features, the Jailbreaking Enhancer raises Attack Success Rate with relative improvement (RI) ranging +5.10% to +143.36% across LLMs and benchmarks (e.g., Qwen ASR 23.66%->28.01% on the paper's own dataset, RI +18.38%; Baichuan2 on TwinBreak 11.00%->26.77%, RI +143.36%; LLaMA3 on StrongREJECT 1.67%->3.59%, RI +115.38%). An ablation against length-matched controls (LenCtrl/LenCtrl-dagger using padding/ignore tokens at comparable token counts, ~144-146 tokens) shows those baselines give negligible or negative ASR change (e.g., LLaMA-3 LenCtrl: -3.30%), while the causal-feature-targeted enhancer still gains +5.10% to +18.38%, indicating the gain is attributable to the specific causal content, not prompt length. The Guardrail Advisor outperforms the non-causal Vanilla Extractor baseline on intent-reconstruction metrics: BLEU-4 11.34 vs 7.65, ROUGE-Avg 21.67 vs 16.23 (Table XI). In a simulated simultaneous attack/defense game, the Advisor suppresses Enhanced-attack ASR to 5.00% (own dataset) and 0.13% (StrongREJECT), versus 28.01%/12.82% without defense.
core_contribution: The paper proposes Causal Analyst, a framework that treats LLM jailbreaking as a causal-discovery problem over human-readable prompt features rather than a correlation/latent-representation problem. It (1) builds a labeled dataset of 35,000 jailbreak attempts (100 templates x 50 harmful queries, tested on 7 LLMs) annotated with 37 human-readable prompt features spanning Encryption, Hijacking, and Setting attack families; (2) jointly trains an LLM (Qwen2.5-7B) prompt classifier and a GNN-based causal graph learner (DAG-GNN) via a 3-stage cross-training procedure with an alignment loss, ELBO loss, and reconstruction loss, fusing latent LLM features with explicit human-readable features (additive/multiplicative/attentive fusion) to reconstruct causal pathways from prompt features to jailbreak response types (AH, AW, AR, AG, AN); and (3) demonstrates two downstream applications built on the learned causal graph: a Jailbreaking Enhancer (Qwen2.5-7B fine-tuned via causal-intervention-based data synthesis) that amplifies causally-identified features in a failed prompt to raise attack success, and a Guardrail Advisor (Qwen2.5-7B fine-tuned to strip 'causal noise' and extract true user intent) intended to improve detection of obfuscated malicious queries.
threat_model: Black-box, template-based jailbreak attacks against instruction-tuned/aligned LLMs (Qwen 7B/14B/72B, Baichuan2-13B, LLaMA3-8B, GLM4-9B, GPT-4o). The attacker crafts prompts using encryption-, hijacking-, or setting-based template families (defined in Table I) wrapped around harmful queries, without access to model weights, gradients, or logits. Success is strictly defined as eliciting Answer Harmfulness (AH) -- content violating safety policy -- explicitly excluding mere refusal-bypass or warning/guidance responses (AW/AR/AG/AN) that do not contain harmful content. The paper explicitly scopes itself to template-based (not gradient- or optimization-based/white-box) attacks, stating this as a limitation in its own Limitations section.
limitations: Authors' own stated limitations: (1) analysis is restricted to template-based attacks chosen for their interpretable/annotatable structure -- gradient-based and optimization-based (white-box) jailbreak attacks are explicitly out of scope and left as future work; (2) loss-weighting hyperparameters (lambda_a, lambda_r, lambda_c) were manually tuned rather than automatically optimized; (3) generalization of the causal patterns to emerging model architectures and multimodal inputs is untested and left to future work. Additional caveats evident from the text itself: feature labeling relies partly on GPT-4o for semantic/stylistic features, introducing a dependency on that model's own judgments as ground truth; the response-classification confusion matrix (Figure 4) shows substantial off-diagonal confusion between AR/AW (0.73-0.76) and AG/AW (0.81) categories, meaning the 5-way response taxonomy used as the causal outcome variable is imperfectly separable; GPT-4o was omitted from ASR experiments 'due to cost concerns,' and Qwen-7B/72B were omitted 'to streamline the experimental workload,' narrowing the evaluated model set for the enhancer experiments (Table IX covers Qwen, Baichuan2, LLaMA3, GLM4 only).

datasets_used: Self-constructed Jailbreaking Attempts Dataset D: 35,000 jailbreak attempts, 100 templates x 50 harmful queries x 7 target LLMs (Qwen-7B/14B/72B, Baichuan2-13B, LLaMA3-8B, GLM4-9B, GPT-4o), 37 human-readable prompt features, 30,000 train / 5,000 validation split
Dc: subset of D for classifier-head training
Dg: subset of D for causal graph learning
Dj: causal-intervention-augmented parallel corpus for the Jailbreaking Enhancer (weakened-prompt -> original-prompt pairs)
Da: prompt -> true-intent pairs for the Guardrail Advisor
StrongREJECT (small version) -- public benchmark used to test Enhancer out-of-distribution generalization
TwinBreak (malicious-prompt subset only) -- public benchmark used to test Enhancer out-of-distribution generalization
quantitative_results: Prompt classification (individual training): AP 0.9070, HS 0.9129, F1 0.7973, AUC 0.9294, RL 0.0707, OE 0.1602
Prompt classification (cross-training with graph learner): AP 0.9115, HS 0.9180, F1 0.8108, AUC 0.9328, RL 0.0674, OE 0.1525
Causal graph edge connectivity: Causal Analyst 12.07% (213 edges) vs PC 9.86% (174 edges) vs DirectLiNGAM 21.60% (381 edges); 628 edges allowed (35.60%) out of 1,764 total possible
Shuffled-label validation: SHD=135, edges drop 213->78, F1 drops 0.8108->0.6611, LAlign 0.0124->0.6834, LELBO 0.0138->0.6558

Length-control ablation on Qwen2.5 (~137 baseline tokens): Original ASR 23.66%; LenCtrl-dagger (~144 tok) 23.81% (RI +0.65%); LenCtrl (~144 tok) 24.68% (RI +4.31%); Ours (~146 tok) 28.01% (RI +18.38%). On LLaMA-3: Original 14.34%; LenCtrl-dagger 13.98% (RI -2.49%); LenCtrl 13.87% (RI -3.30%); Ours 15.07% (RI +5.10%)
Guardrail Advisor vs Vanilla Extractor: BLEU-4 11.34 vs 7.65; ROUGE-1 29.36 vs 23.32; ROUGE-2 9.80 vs 6.53; ROUGE-L 25.86 vs 19.86; ROUGE-Avg 21.67 vs 16.23

Simultaneous attack/defense game (Raw->Enhanced ASR without defense, and Enhanced ASR with Advisor defense): own dataset 23.66%->28.01% (no defense) vs 5.00% (with Advisor); StrongREJECT 6.67%->12.82% vs 0.13%; TwinBreak 12.00%->18.69% vs 2.00%->3.85%
Feature fusion ablation: Additive AP 0.851/AUC 0.879; Multiplicative AP 0.881/AUC 0.905 (selected default); Attentive AP 0.891/AUC 0.917 but LELBO 4.243 (destabilized graph learning)

Training efficiency: Stage 1 Classifier 5.06M params, 44.65 min, 18.73GB peak GPU; Stage 2 Graph Learner 5.20M params, 44.74 min, 18.77GB; Stage 3 Joint Alignment 5.22M params, 44.95 min, 18.77GB (single RTX 4090)
baselines_compared: PC algorithm (constraint-based causal discovery, via causal-learn library)
DirectLiNGAM (causal discovery algorithm, via causal-learn library)
LenCtrl-dagger (prefixes model-specific pad tokens, e.g. <|endoftext|> for Qwen2.5, <|end_of_text|> for LLaMA-3) -- length-matched ablation baseline
Vanilla Extractor (Qwen2.5-7B fine-tuned on Da without access to the learned causal graph) -- baseline for Guardrail Advisor
